candidateIssues.details: The error boundary for the Research Hub displays both 'Refresh Page' and 'Retry' buttons. These likely perform very similar actions from a user's perspective, adding unnecessary cognitive load and violating the principle of earned complexity.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Consolidate into a single 'Try Again' button. If they perform distinct technical actions (e.g., soft re-render vs hard page reload), choose the most robust option and remove the other.

candidateIssues.details: In the 'For You' feed, the bottom right article under 'AI BRIEF' displays a summary reading: 'Summary includes what happened, why it matters, and a key implication. 2-3 sentences.' This is clearly an LLM prompt instruction or placeholder text leaking into the production UI, which breaks user trust.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Fix the data pipeline to ensure the generated summary is extracted correctly, or implement a fallback mechanism that hides the summary block if valid content is missing.

candidateIssues.details: The LinkedIn Posts archive shows two posts for 'MONDAY, FEBRUARY 2, 2026' with identical body content ('antfu/skills - Anthony Fu's curated collection...') but different tags ('Tech Builder' vs 'Investor') and timestamps. This looks like a data generation bug or a failure to deduplicate.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Investigate the backend logic for generating or fetching these posts to prevent duplicates. If they are intentional variations, visually group them or clarify why they are distinct.

candidateIssues.details: The settings modal displays a 'Current Session' block at the top, and then lists 'Active Sessions' below, which again includes 'This Device'. This repetition clutters the interface without adding value.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the separate 'Current Session' block. Rely on the 'Active Sessions' list and ensure the current device is clearly highlighted (e.g., with the existing 'Active' badge).

candidateIssues.details: The 'Run Benchmark' button in the header is horizontally truncated on mobile viewports, making the primary call-to-action difficult to identify and interact with.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use an icon-only button for mobile or move the action to a secondary header row.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Retry' button in the 'failed to load' component has extremely low contrast against its background, failing WCAG accessibility standards and making the recovery path invisible to many users.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the background lightness of the secondary button or use an outlined style with higher contrast text.

candidateIssues.details: Activity durations use inconsistent formatting. Short durations use '2m' while longer ones use '1h 0m'. The inclusion of '0m' adds unnecessary cognitive load.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Standardize on a single format (e.g., '1h' or '60m') and omit zero-value units.

candidateIssues.details: The breadcrumb uses the engineering abbreviation 'PR Suggestions' while the page title uses 'Pull Request Suggestions'. This violates the 'Kill Jargon' principle.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Update breadcrumbs to match the full user-facing page title.

candidateIssues.details: The calendar sidebar displays a raw system timezone string ('System (America/Los_Angeles)'), which feels like unpolished internal metadata rather than a user-facing feature.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Format the timezone to a user-friendly string like 'Pacific Time' or hide the system label entirely.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Unknown' category is the dominant source in the performance chart. This indicates a data-wiring issue that provides no value to the user and clutters the view.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Filter out 'Unknown' sources or group them into an 'Other' category at the bottom of the list.

candidateIssues.details: The Floating Action Button (FAB) consistently overlaps primary content and text in mobile views, such as the 'Agent Integration' card and the 'Morning Dossier' summary.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Add bottom padding to the scroll container to ensure content can be scrolled past the FAB's fixed position.

candidateIssues.details: The search bar placeholder on the home screen ends with a double period '..', which appears as a typo rather than a deliberate ellipsis.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change to a single period or a proper ellipsis (...).

candidateIssues.details: The 'Trending' section in the Signals view lists technologies without commas or proper spacing, making the list difficult to parse.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use bullet points, chips, or at least commas to separate list items.

candidateIssues.details: The '11 sessions' label is redundant as the specific status counts (10 completed, 1 failed) are provided immediately next to it.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the total count to reduce visual noise, as the sum is easily inferred from the status breakdown.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Model selection dropdown fails to open
candidateIssues.details: Clicking the model selection dropdown button (e.g., 'Qwen3 Coder (Free)') does not display the menu of available models as expected.

candidateIssues.header: P2 User profile menu fails to open
candidateIssues.details: Clicking the user profile avatar in the top right corner does not trigger the expected dropdown menu, showing no visual response to the interaction.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Create new item button fails to trigger action
candidateIssues.details: The floating action button (blue '+' icon) in the bottom right does not open a creation menu or modal upon interaction.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Interactive elements fail to provide visual feedback or open menus
candidateIssues.details: Interacting with the search bar, 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and user profile menu does not trigger any visual state changes, focus indicators, or expected popovers/drawers. The UI remains identical across all interaction states, indicating a lack of responsiveness.

candidateIssues.header: P3 Contradictory scoring information in repository cards
candidateIssues.details: Repository cards display a numerical score (e.g., 'Score 100') while simultaneously displaying a footer message stating 'Ranked by popularity (scoring unavailable)', which provides conflicting information to the user.

candidateIssues.header: P2 [Interaction] User profile menu does not open
candidateIssues.details: Clicking on the user profile icon ('U' in the top right) does not trigger the expected dropdown menu or overlay, as the UI remains unchanged in the 'After' state.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Interactive filter dropdowns are non-responsive
candidateIssues.details: Clicking on the 'All Time' and 'All Rounds' filter dropdowns does not trigger the display of the selection menus. The UI state remains identical to the base state after the interaction, indicating the dropdowns are non-functional.

candidateIssues.header: P2 [User profile menu fails to open or show active state]
candidateIssues.details: Interacting with the user profile icon in the top right corner does not trigger a menu or provide any visual feedback (such as a focus ring or background change), unlike other interactive elements in the app.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Floating action button overlaps sidebar content
candidateIssues.details: The blue '+' floating action button in the bottom right corner overlaps the 'Synthesis', 'Briefing', and 'Signals' labels in the Pulse section, obscuring the text and potentially blocking user interaction.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Overlapping text labels in sidebar chart
candidateIssues.details: In the top right sidebar chart (labeled 'Feb 2026'), multiple text labels for data points (e.g., 'Leonxtnx/taste-skill', 'ShinMegamiBoson/Op...') are overlapping, making them difficult to read.

[00:01:40] The 'Autonomous Operations' section in the AI Assistants dashboard loads several seconds after the rest of the page, causing a significant layout shift that pushes the 'Available Agents' list downwards.

videoSummary: The application presents a sleek, dark-mode aesthetic suitable for a professional tool, but suffers from several polish and data-handling issues that detract from a premium feel. Key areas for improvement include removing leaked placeholder text in content feeds, consolidating redundant UI actions in error states, and cleaning up duplicate data rendering. Addressing these will elevate the perceive
screenSummary: The interface exhibits a high level of visual polish and a clear 'earned complexity' aesthetic, but it is currently marred by technical jargon leakage, inconsistent grammar in data formatting, and critical mobile responsiveness issues where primary actions are truncated. Refinement of empty states and action button contrast is required to meet professional standards.

aspiration.summary: NodeBench AI exhibits a sophisticated, high-density design language that successfully balances complexity with clarity. It closely follows the 'Linear-grade' aesthetic, though minor refinements in empty state polish and consistent card padding would elevate it to top-tier industry standards.

issues.header: P2 Missing visual feedback and state changes for header interactions
issues.details: Interacting with the search bar, 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and user profile icon does not trigger any visual feedback (e.g., focus rings, hover states, active states) or open expected UI components like dropdowns or drawers. The UI remains static despite the user interactions.

issues.header: P3 Missing visual feedback for header buttons
issues.details: The 'Research' and 'Assistant' buttons in the top header do not show any visual change (such as a background color shift or border) during hover or active states, providing no feedback to the user that the interaction was registered.

issues.header: P3 Missing hover/active visual states for header elements
issues.details: The 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and user profile icon do not show any visual feedback (such as a background highlight or dropdown menu) when interacted with, unlike the search bar which correctly shows a focus state.

issues.header: P2 [Interactivity] Header interactive elements fail to trigger menus or drawers
issues.details: The Date Picker, Research button, Assistant button, and User Profile menu do not show any visual response, menus, or drawers after interaction. The UI state remains identical across all 'After' interaction screenshots, indicating these components are non-functional or lack necessary visual feedback states.

issues.header: P2 Interactive elements fail to respond to user clicks
issues.details: The filter dropdowns ('All Time', 'All Rounds') and 'Show details' links on the funding cards do not show any visual change, open menus, or expanded content after interaction. The UI state remains identical across all screenshots despite the recorded user actions.

issues.header: P3 Inconsistent badge layout on cards with long titles
issues.details: In the 'Tech Investor Battery Ventures' card, the 'single-source' badge wraps to a new line due to the long title. This creates inconsistent vertical spacing and alignment compared to other cards where badges remain on the same line as the title.

issues.header: P3 Missing interactive states for primary actions
issues.details: The 'add new entry' floating action button, 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and search bar do not show any visual feedback (hover, active, or focus states) after user interaction. This makes the interface feel unresponsive.

issues.header: P2 [Interactive element] User profile icon does not show active state or menu after interaction
issues.details: Clicking or interacting with the user profile icon (top right) does not trigger a visual change, such as a dropdown menu or an active state highlight, which is expected for this type of element.

issues.header: P2 AI Capabilities widget overlaps sidebar content
issues.details: The circular 'GITHUB 100% of sources' graphic is overlapping the 'AI Capabilities' section header and the progress bars for Reasoning, Uptime, and Safety, making the text difficult to read.

issues.header: P2 Date badge overlaps text in sidebar
issues.details: The 'Feb 2026' badge at the top of the right sidebar is overlapping the 'VS. RELIA...' text.

judgeDetails.reasoning: Context identifies overlapping chart labels as artifacts of screenshot compression.
Act Coverage is explicitly listed as established domain terminology for this audience.
Specific metric labeling nuances are intentional for technical power-user context.
Different icons per view are intentional context cues for empty states.
Repeating metadata in dense feeds provides individual card context for power users.

judgeDetails.issue: P2Technical jargon and poor time formatting
judgeDetails.reasoning: Technical terminology and specific time formatting are intentional for power users.
Greeting uses a placeholder fallback string common in demo environments.

judgeDetails.issue: P1Lack of craft in mobile Signals view
judgeDetails.reasoning: High density and repeated navigation are intentional for this domain.
Minimalist metric display without labels supports intentional layout density.
Persistent sidebars are a deliberate architectural choice for power-user workflows.
Compact layouts and touch targets are intentional for power users.

candidateIssues.details: In the Research Hub Briefing view, the line chart has data point labels that overlap significantly, making them unreadable and creating visual noise.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement label collision detection to hide, stagger, or abbreviate overlapping labels. Alternatively, show labels only on hover.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Briefing Snapshot' section uses the term 'Act coverage'. It is unclear if 'Act' refers to 'Actionable', a specific legislative act, or something else, adding cognitive load.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Expand the abbreviation or use a more descriptive, plain-English term (e.g., 'Actionable Coverage').

candidateIssues.details: The metric shows 'Confidence' as the main label and '62% accuracy score' below it. 'Confidence' and 'accuracy score' are somewhat redundant in this context.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Simplify to 'Confidence Score' or just 'Accuracy' to reduce unnecessary words.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Review Queue' empty state uses a heartbeat/pulse icon. While visually interesting, it doesn't strongly correlate with the concept of a 'review' or a 'queue'.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace the pulse icon with an icon more representative of tasks, reviews, or an inbox (e.g., a checkmark list, an empty tray).

candidateIssues.details: In the LinkedIn Posts view, every post repeats 'Date: 2026-02-02'. Since the view is already grouped by date ('MONDAY, FEBRUARY 2, 2026'), repeating the date inside every card is redundant and wastes vertical space.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the redundant 'Date:' line from individual post cards when they are already grouped under a date header.

candidateIssues.header: P2Technical jargon and poor time formatting
candidateIssues.details: The 'Executive Synthesis' card uses '209m ago' and '(cached)'. Measuring time in hundreds of minutes is difficult for users to parse, and 'cached' is internal engineering jargon that adds no value to the end-user experience.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Format durations into hours/minutes (e.g., '3.5h ago') and remove technical status indicators like 'cached' from the primary UI.

candidateIssues.details: The greeting 'Good morning, there' feels like a broken template or an unintentional placeholder. It lacks the 'calm and intentional' feel of a premium product.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change to 'Good morning' or 'Good morning, [User Name]'.

candidateIssues.header: P1Lack of craft in mobile Signals view
candidateIssues.details: The mobile 'Signals' view lacks visual hierarchy and looks like a raw text dump. Repetitive 'Dashboard' links and ambiguous labels like 'GitHub 11' create high cognitive load and feel unfinished compared to the desktop version.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Apply the card-based layout used in the desktop 'Latest Updates' view to the mobile screen to improve legibility and touch affordance.

candidateIssues.details: Signal cards display percentage values (e.g., '65%', '62%') without labels or tooltips. Without context, these numbers are meaningless to the user.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Add a label or icon to indicate what the percentage represents (e.g., Match Score, Confidence, or Sentiment).

candidateIssues.details: The Documents view includes a large, persistent calendar sidebar. Unless the application is primarily a scheduling tool, this element adds significant visual noise and 'unearned complexity' to a file management task.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Collapse the calendar into a toggleable panel or remove it if document activity is not strictly date-dependent.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Run' button in the mobile Scenarios list is small and placed very close to descriptive text, making it difficult to trigger accurately on mobile devices.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the button height to at least 44px and add more padding between the button and the surrounding text.

[02:03] When the page is refreshed or re-navigated, the repository cards abruptly lose all metadata (scores, descriptions, tags) and revert to a minimal state before disappearing and re-loading. This creates a significant visual flicker and layout instability.

candidateIssues.header: P2 [Significant layout reflow during content hydration]
candidateIssues.details: [04:08] The 'Executive Synthesis' section loads its content after the rest of the page, causing a large vertical layout shift that pushes all subsequent dashboard elements (Top Signals, Sources, etc.) down. This is disruptive to the user's initial scan of the page.

falsePositives.details: The 'Source Performance' list displays '1 items' for entries with a single item, violating basic grammatical consistency.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper to conditionally render 'item' or 'items' based on the count.

falsePositives.details: In the Source Performance chart, 'Unknown' shows 36 items with a full-width bar, while 'All Sources' shows only 11 items with a shorter bar. Logically, 'All Sources' should represent the aggregate total or be renamed if it refers to a specific subset, as the current labeling is confusing.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Ensure the 'All Sources' category correctly aggregates all data points or rename it to 'Other Sources' if that is the intent.

falsePositives.details: In the Research Hub Briefing view, the line chart has data point labels that overlap significantly, making them unreadable and creating visual noise.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement label collision detection to hide, stagger, or abbreviate overlapping labels. Alternatively, show labels only on hover.

falsePositives.details: The 'Briefing Snapshot' section uses the term 'Act coverage'. It is unclear if 'Act' refers to 'Actionable', a specific legislative act, or something else, adding cognitive load.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Expand the abbreviation or use a more descriptive, plain-English term (e.g., 'Actionable Coverage').

falsePositives.details: The metric shows 'Confidence' as the main label and '62% accuracy score' below it. 'Confidence' and 'accuracy score' are somewhat redundant in this context.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Simplify to 'Confidence Score' or just 'Accuracy' to reduce unnecessary words.

falsePositives.details: The 'Review Queue' empty state uses a heartbeat/pulse icon. While visually interesting, it doesn't strongly correlate with the concept of a 'review' or a 'queue'.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace the pulse icon with an icon more representative of tasks, reviews, or an inbox (e.g., a checkmark list, an empty tray).

falsePositives.details: In the LinkedIn Posts view, every post repeats 'Date: 2026-02-02'. Since the view is already grouped by date ('MONDAY, FEBRUARY 2, 2026'), repeating the date inside every card is redundant and wastes vertical space.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the redundant 'Date:' line from individual post cards when they are already grouped under a date header.

falsePositives.header: P2Technical jargon and poor time formatting
falsePositives.details: The 'Executive Synthesis' card uses '209m ago' and '(cached)'. Measuring time in hundreds of minutes is difficult for users to parse, and 'cached' is internal engineering jargon that adds no value to the end-user experience.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Format durations into hours/minutes (e.g., '3.5h ago') and remove technical status indicators like 'cached' from the primary UI.

falsePositives.details: The greeting 'Good morning, there' feels like a broken template or an unintentional placeholder. It lacks the 'calm and intentional' feel of a premium product.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change to 'Good morning' or 'Good morning, [User Name]'.

falsePositives.header: P1Lack of craft in mobile Signals view
falsePositives.details: The mobile 'Signals' view lacks visual hierarchy and looks like a raw text dump. Repetitive 'Dashboard' links and ambiguous labels like 'GitHub 11' create high cognitive load and feel unfinished compared to the desktop version.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Apply the card-based layout used in the desktop 'Latest Updates' view to the mobile screen to improve legibility and touch affordance.

falsePositives.details: Signal cards display percentage values (e.g., '65%', '62%') without labels or tooltips. Without context, these numbers are meaningless to the user.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Add a label or icon to indicate what the percentage represents (e.g., Match Score, Confidence, or Sentiment).

falsePositives.details: The Documents view includes a large, persistent calendar sidebar. Unless the application is primarily a scheduling tool, this element adds significant visual noise and 'unearned complexity' to a file management task.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Collapse the calendar into a toggleable panel or remove it if document activity is not strictly date-dependent.

falsePositives.details: The 'Run' button in the mobile Scenarios list is small and placed very close to descriptive text, making it difficult to trigger accurately on mobile devices.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the button height to at least 44px and add more padding between the button and the surrounding text.

falsePositives.header: P2 Missing visual feedback and state changes for header interactions
falsePositives.details: Interacting with the search bar, 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and user profile icon does not trigger any visual feedback (e.g., focus rings, hover states, active states) or open expected UI components like dropdowns or drawers. The UI remains static despite the user interactions.

falsePositives.header: P3 Missing visual feedback for header buttons
falsePositives.details: The 'Research' and 'Assistant' buttons in the top header do not show any visual change (such as a background color shift or border) during hover or active states, providing no feedback to the user that the interaction was registered.

falsePositives.header: P3 Missing hover/active visual states for header elements
falsePositives.details: The 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and user profile icon do not show any visual feedback (such as a background highlight or dropdown menu) when interacted with, unlike the search bar which correctly shows a focus state.

falsePositives.header: P2 [Interactivity] Header interactive elements fail to trigger menus or drawers
falsePositives.details: The Date Picker, Research button, Assistant button, and User Profile menu do not show any visual response, menus, or drawers after interaction. The UI state remains identical across all 'After' interaction screenshots, indicating these components are non-functional or lack necessary visual feedback states.

falsePositives.header: P2 Interactive elements fail to respond to user clicks
falsePositives.details: The filter dropdowns ('All Time', 'All Rounds') and 'Show details' links on the funding cards do not show any visual change, open menus, or expanded content after interaction. The UI state remains identical across all screenshots despite the recorded user actions.

falsePositives.header: P3 Inconsistent badge layout on cards with long titles
falsePositives.details: In the 'Tech Investor Battery Ventures' card, the 'single-source' badge wraps to a new line due to the long title. This creates inconsistent vertical spacing and alignment compared to other cards where badges remain on the same line as the title.

falsePositives.header: P3 Missing interactive states for primary actions
falsePositives.details: The 'add new entry' floating action button, 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and search bar do not show any visual feedback (hover, active, or focus states) after user interaction. This makes the interface feel unresponsive.

falsePositives.header: P2 [Interactive element] User profile icon does not show active state or menu after interaction
falsePositives.details: Clicking or interacting with the user profile icon (top right) does not trigger a visual change, such as a dropdown menu or an active state highlight, which is expected for this type of element.

falsePositives.header: P2 AI Capabilities widget overlaps sidebar content
falsePositives.details: The circular 'GITHUB 100% of sources' graphic is overlapping the 'AI Capabilities' section header and the progress bars for Reasoning, Uptime, and Safety, making the text difficult to read.

falsePositives.header: P2 Date badge overlaps text in sidebar
falsePositives.details: The 'Feb 2026' badge at the top of the right sidebar is overlapping the 'VS. RELIA...' text.

falsePositives.header: P2 Chart labels overlap in sidebar
falsePositives.details: In the line chart at the top of the right sidebar, labels like 'LeonLnx/taste-skill' and 'ShinMegamiBoson/Op...' are overlapping each other and the chart lines, making them illegible.

falsePositives.header: P2 [Layout] Overlapping content in right sidebar
falsePositives.details: Multiple UI elements in the right sidebar are overlapping, making them illegible. Specifically, the 'Feb 2026 VS. RELIA...' text overlaps with 'Last 7 days', and the 'GITHUB 100% of sources' graphic overlaps with the 'AI Capabilities' section.

videoSummary: The application demonstrates a strong, dark-themed aesthetic with a generally clean layout. However, there are areas where data visualization becomes cluttered, specifically with overlapping chart labels. Additionally, some terminology feels slightly opaque or redundant, and there are opportunities to streamline repetitive information in feed views to enhance scannability and reduce cognitive load
screenSummary: The application exhibits a high level of visual sophistication and excellent theme parity between light and dark modes. However, it is currently hindered by inconsistent data visualization logic, minor grammatical errors in pluralization, and a lack of design rigor in high-density mobile views which feel like unformatted data dumps rather than intentional interfaces.

judgeDetails.reasoning: Narrative terminology is established domain language for the target audience.
Intentional navigation hierarchy and dual entry points for dense power-user tools.
Perfect scores are placeholder data in the demo environment.

judgeDetails.issue: P3Low contrast on secondary feed text
judgeDetails.reasoning: Contrast ratios meet WCAG AA standards; perceived low contrast is intentional.
Pluralization logic is already fixed; this is a stale screenshot observation.
Casing inconsistencies in categories are data-level artifacts of the mock/demo environment.
Funding stages are standard domain terminology for the target AI/SaaS audience.
Truncation claims are typically misreads of compressed screenshots or intentional ellipsis.
Consistent empty state messaging across different timeframes is an intentional design choice.
Tool-call eval is standard industry terminology for AI model performance benchmarking.
Casing hierarchy is a stylistic choice for differentiating content types in dense feeds.
Vision models frequently misinterpret character spacing and punctuation in compressed UI screenshots.
Minute-based durations provide higher precision for technical users monitoring real-time operations.

falsePositivePatterns: P3Low contrast on secondary feed text

candidateIssues.details: The Briefing section utilizes narrative terminology such as 'Story Arc', 'Act I - Setup', and 'Act II - Rising Action'. This is internal or highly specific jargon that likely does not align with a standard user's mental model for data briefings or research reports, thereby adding unnecessary cognitive load and violating the 'kill jargon' principle.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace narrative terms with standard, descriptive reporting terminology (e.g., 'Overview', 'Key Signals', 'Deep Dives', 'Structure').

candidateIssues.details: When the 'Home' item is selected in the primary sidebar, the main content area title displays 'Research Hub' and the breadcrumb reads 'Home > Research Hub'. This creates ambiguity regarding the site's hierarchy—it is unclear if the user is on the 'Home' page or a sub-page called 'Research Hub'.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Align the nomenclature. If 'Research Hub' is the default home view, rename the sidebar item to 'Research Hub' or change the page title to 'Home'. Ensure breadcrumbs accurately reflect the active sidebar selection.

candidateIssues.details: The 'AI Capabilities' widget displays perfect 100% scores for 'Reasoning', 'Uptime', and 'Safety'. This appears to be static placeholder data or marketing fluff rather than actionable, dynamic user data. It lacks context and reduces the perceived utility of the dashboard.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the widget if it does not provide real, dynamic data. If the data is real, provide tooltips or context explaining how these metrics are calculated to build trust.

candidateIssues.header: P3Low contrast on secondary feed text
candidateIssues.details: In the 'For You' feed, secondary metadata text (e.g., 'Trending on Hacker News...') has relatively low contrast against the dark background. This may impact legibility for users with visual impairments or in brightly lit environments.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Slightly increase the brightness or opacity of the secondary text color token in the dark theme to ensure it meets WCAG AA contrast guidelines.

candidateIssues.details: The 'For You' feed displays '1 comments' for a post, violating basic grammar rules.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper function to handle singular/plural cases (e.g., count === 1 ? 'comment' : 'comments').

candidateIssues.details: The Source Performance list shows both 'Unknown' and 'unknown' as separate categories, suggesting a lack of data normalization.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Normalize source strings to title case or lowercase before aggregation.

candidateIssues.details: Labels like 'series-a', 'series-b', and 'series-d-plus' appear to be internal database keys or engineering tags rather than user-friendly names.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Map internal identifiers to human-readable display names.

candidateIssues.details: In the Source Performance list, the last item is cut off, showing only '2 i' instead of the full count and label.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Ensure container height or overflow settings allow for full visibility of list items.

candidateIssues.details: The calendar sidebar repeats 'Nothing upcoming' three times for different timeframes (Today, This Week, etc.), creating visual noise.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Consolidate empty states or use more varied, helpful messaging for different timeframes.

candidateIssues.details: 'Tool-call eval' is an engineering-centric term that may not be immediately clear to all users.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace with a more descriptive, user-facing term like 'Function Calling Accuracy'.

candidateIssues.details: Some headlines use sentence case while others use title case, leading to a disorganized feel.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Standardize on one casing style across all feed items.

candidateIssues.details: There is an awkward space before the slash in the benchmark score '90 /100'.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the space before the slash for a tighter look.

candidateIssues.details: '124m ago' is used for a duration over two hours. Standard UX patterns suggest switching to hours after 60 minutes.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Format durations over 60 minutes as '2h 4m ago'.

falsePositives.details: The search input shows 'Al agents'. The use of a lowercase 'l' instead of an uppercase 'I' for 'AI' is a common typo that looks unprofessional.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Correct the typo to 'AI agents'.

videoSummary: The application presents a dense, information-rich dark mode interface tailored for research and analysis. While the overall layout is structured and functional, the experience is hindered by the use of confusing internal narrative jargon ('Story Arc', 'Acts') that increases cognitive load. Additionally, minor navigational inconsistencies between the sidebar and breadcrumbs, along with the presenc
screenSummary: The interface demonstrates a sophisticated aesthetic but suffers from micro-copy inconsistencies and data presentation issues. Frequent pluralization errors, inconsistent casing, and the presence of internal-sounding data strings (jargon) detract from the premium feel. Mobile layouts are generally functional but occasionally feel cramped or lack clear visual separation between interactive elements

aspiration.summary: NodeBench AI demonstrates exceptional craft, successfully blending Linear's density with Vercel's clean aesthetic. It feels like a professional power-user tool, though some complex views could benefit from sharper internal hierarchy and more intentional negative space to reduce cognitive load.

judgeDetails.reasoning: Intentional density for power users with 30+ routes is a core design principle.

judgeDetails.issue: P1Conflicting dates in header and timeline
judgeDetails.reasoning: Date mismatches in preview environment stem from static placeholder data.
Vision models misinterpret Recharts positioning in compressed screenshots as overlaps.
Stale observation; pluralization logic is already implemented in the codebase.
Narrative terminology like 'Acts' is an intentional domain-specific design choice.
Compact badge placement is an intentional choice for high-density navigation.

judgeDetails.issue: P1Duplicate categories due to case sensitivity
judgeDetails.reasoning: Duplicate categories arise from unnormalized mock data in the demo environment.
Pluralization logic is already fixed; this is a stale screenshot observation.
Kebab-case labels are raw keys from the mock dataset, not code.
Lowercase metadata is an intentional stylistic choice for the activity feed.
Intentional repetition for navigation context in dense power-user interfaces.
Kebab-case is standard for GitHub tags and expected by technical users.
Cache status is relevant technical metadata for AI researchers and engineers.
Context confirms codebase uses standard ellipses; vision model misread the punctuation.
Zero-values and specific phrasing are artifacts of the placeholder data environment.

falsePositivePatterns: P1Conflicting dates in header and timeline
P1Duplicate categories due to case sensitivity

candidateIssues.details: The left sidebar contains over 10 items under the 'MORE' section, mixing core features, specific integrations (GitHub Explorer, LinkedIn Posts), and administrative settings (Usage & Costs). This lacks hierarchy, overwhelms the user, and violates the principle of earned complexity.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Group secondary tools under a single 'Apps' or 'Integrations' menu, and move administrative items like 'Usage & Costs' to a user settings dropdown or a dedicated settings area.

candidateIssues.header: P1Conflicting dates in header and timeline
candidateIssues.details: The timeline pill at the top indicates 'Feb 22' is the active selection, but the main page header reads 'Today's Briefing FEB 23, 2026'. This creates cognitive dissonance and reduces trust in the accuracy of the data presented.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Ensure the selected timeline state strictly drives the content header date, or clarify the relationship between the timeline selection and the briefing date.

candidateIssues.details: In the right sidebar of the Research Hub, the text '100% of sources' inside the circular donut chart overlaps the chart's stroke. This looks unpolished and makes the text harder to read.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Adjust the font size, line height, or the radius of the donut chart to ensure the text is fully contained within the negative space of the graphic.

candidateIssues.details: In the 'For You' feed, a subtext reads '1 comments'. This breaks the illusion of a crafted, intelligent system and shows a lack of attention to micro-details.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Add pluralization logic to the string formatter (e.g., `count === 1 ? 'comment' : 'comments'`).

candidateIssues.details: The Brief Structure section uses terms like 'Act I — Setup', 'Act II — Rising Action', and 'Act III — Deep Dives'. While creative, this adds unnecessary cognitive load for users expecting straightforward data analysis terminology.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace theatrical terms with clear, descriptive labels like 'Overview', 'Key Signals', and 'In-Depth Analysis'.

candidateIssues.details: The '9+' red notification badge on the 'Research' button in the top right header feels slightly misaligned and visually crowded against the icon.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Adjust the absolute positioning of the badge to sit cleanly on the top-right corner of the icon, adding a slight background border (mask) to separate it from the underlying shape.

candidateIssues.header: P1Duplicate categories due to case sensitivity
candidateIssues.details: The Source Performance chart shows separate bars for 'Unknown' and 'unknown', indicating a lack of data normalization in the analytics pipeline.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Normalize all source strings to title case or lowercase before aggregation.

candidateIssues.details: The 'For You Feed' displays '1 comments' instead of the grammatically correct '1 comment'.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper to handle singular vs. plural counts.

candidateIssues.details: Analytics labels like 'series-a' and 'series-d-plus' use kebab-case, which feels like raw database keys rather than user-facing labels.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Map internal keys to human-readable display names (e.g., 'Series A').

candidateIssues.details: The activity feed uses lowercase for '11 sessions', '10 completed', and '1 failed', while the rest of the UI uses Title or Sentence case for headers and labels.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Capitalize the first letter of these status indicators to match the surrounding UI.

candidateIssues.details: In the mobile Signals view, the word 'Dashboard' is repeated multiple times as a link within a single paragraph, creating visual clutter and high cognitive load.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove inline 'Dashboard' links and provide a single, clear primary action button at the bottom of the card.

candidateIssues.details: Repository tags are displayed in raw kebab-case (e.g., 'awesome-list', 'tutorial-code'). This breaks the 'Kill jargon' rule by exposing technical formatting.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Transform kebab-case strings into Title Case with spaces for display.

candidateIssues.details: The research summary shows '(cached)' next to the timestamp. This is an implementation detail that adds cognitive load without providing user value.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the '(cached)' label; users generally expect data to be reasonably fresh without needing to know the storage mechanism.

candidateIssues.details: The search/input placeholder on the mobile home screen ends with two periods instead of an ellipsis or a single period.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change '..' to '...' or a single period.

candidateIssues.details: Labels like '0 sec Average engagement' and '0% No clicks recorded yet' feel like sentence fragments and lack clear hierarchy between the metric and the description.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use a standard vertical stack: Large metric on top, smaller descriptive label underneath.

This lacks hierarchy, overwhelms the user, and violates the principle of earned complexity.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Group secondary tools under a single 'Apps' or 'Integrations' menu, and move administrative items like 'Usage & Costs' to a user settings dropdown or a dedicated settings area.

videoSummary: The application presents a powerful, data-dense environment but suffers from navigational bloat and minor polish issues that detract from a calm, intentional user experience. The sidebar is overloaded with disparate tools, lacking clear hierarchy. Additionally, there are noticeable data inconsistencies (date mismatches) and typographical errors (pluralization, text overlaps) that need addressing t
screenSummary: The NodeBench AI interface demonstrates a sophisticated dark-mode aesthetic but suffers from 'data-leakage' where internal engineering structures (kebab-case tags, case-sensitive strings, and technical jargon like 'cached') are exposed to the end-user. There are also several polish issues regarding pluralization and inconsistent capitalization that detract from the 'Craft at every scale' philosoph

aspiration.summary: The application exhibits a high baseline of craft with a clean, data-dense layout that respects the power-user intent. It successfully avoids unnecessary ornamentation, though it currently lacks the aggressive progressive disclosure and typographic refinement seen in top-tier products like Linear or

judgeDetails.reasoning: Casing inconsistencies in demo data are data-level, not code bugs.

judgeDetails.issue: P1Critical Contrast Issue in Dark Mode

candidateIssues.details: The Source Performance list displays 'Unknown' and 'unknown' as separate entries with inconsistent casing, suggesting a data normalization issue.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Normalize all data entries to a consistent casing (e.g., Sentence case) before rendering the list.

candidateIssues.header: P1Critical Contrast Issue in Dark Mode
candidateIssues.details: In the mobile Signals view, the blue text and links on the dark background fail accessibility standards and are nearly illegible.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the lightness of the blue color used for text in dark mode to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios (at least 4.5:1).

candidateIssues.details: The Activity page features two prominent headers ('Public Activity Feed' and 'Activity') in close proximity, which adds unnecessary visual noise.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the secondary 'Activity' header; the primary page title is sufficient to establish context.

candidateIssues.details: The search input on the mobile home screen ends with a double period instead of a standard ellipsis or single period.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change the double period to a single period or a proper ellipsis (...).

candidateIssues.details: The calendar sidebar repeats the phrase 'Nothing upcoming' three times for different timeframes, which feels mechanical and cluttered.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use varied messaging or a single consolidated 'No upcoming items' message for the entire sidebar section.

candidateIssues.details: Labels like 'series-a' and 'series-d-plus' appear to be internal engineering tags or database keys rather than user-friendly source names.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Map internal identifiers to human-readable names or hide them if they do not provide value to the end-user.

candidateIssues.details: The right-hand sidebar in the Research Hub is overly compressed, with text elements and charts nearly overlapping, making it difficult to parse.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase vertical spacing between sections and ensure charts have adequate padding from adjacent text labels.

candidateIssues.details: The GitHub Explorer repeats the same 'scoring unavailable' disclaimer on every single card, which violates the principle of earned complexity.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Move the disclaimer to a single location at the top of the list or include it in a tooltip.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Upgrade to Supporter' button in settings uses a distinct gradient and border style that deviates from the application's standard primary button design.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Align the 'Upgrade' button styling with the standard primary button component used elsewhere in the app.

videoSummary:
{
  "summary": "The application features a dense, information-rich interface tailored for power users, but it suffers from navigation overload and the occasional use of internal jargon. The dark theme is generally well-executed, though modal contrast needs improvement to establish better visual hierarchy. Minor polish issues include cramped tab layouts and abrupt text truncation in feed cards.",
screenSummary: The application demonstrates a sophisticated feature set with a generally clean aesthetic, but suffers from micro-copy inconsistencies, significant contrast issues in dark mode (specifically on mobile), and redundant UI elements that increase cognitive load.

aspiration.summary: NodeBench AI demonstrates a high level of craft with a sophisticated 'power-user' aesthetic that balances data density and clarity. To reach industry-leading status, it needs to refine internal card spacing and adopt more aggressive progressive disclosure to reduce visual noise in complex views.

judgeDetails.reasoning: Repetitive metadata in dense feeds is an intentional design choice for scanning.
High navigation density is intentional for power users managing 30+ routes.

judgeDetails.issue: P2Redundant empty state messaging in Calendar
judgeDetails.reasoning: Contextual labels for different calendar sections are intentional, not redundant.

judgeDetails.issue: P2Cramped typography in Research Hub 'Pulse' section
judgeDetails.reasoning: Compact typography is a core design principle for high-density data platforms.

judgeDetails.issue: P3Inconsistent empty state calls to action
judgeDetails.reasoning: Intentional variation in empty states; some views auto-populate without CTAs.
Technical snake_case is appropriate for the target AI engineering audience.
Pluralization logic is already fixed; observation stems from a stale screenshot.
Dual headers provide necessary context in complex, data-dense power-user tools.
Greeting text is part of the placeholder data in the demo environment.

candidateIssues.details: The 'YCOMBINATOR' label and the string 'Trending on Hacker News with X points and Y comments' are repeated on almost every card. This creates visual noise and violates the 'earned complexity' principle.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the repetitive 'YCOMBINATOR' text and replace it with a small favicon. Condense the meta string to 'X pts • Y cmts'.

candidateIssues.details: The sidebar navigation contains a very long, uncategorized list under the 'MORE' heading, acting as a junk drawer. This makes scanning and finding specific tools difficult.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Group these items into logical sub-categories (e.g., 'Analytics', 'Discovery', 'Admin') or implement a collapsible/pinning system to reduce vertical clutter.

candidateIssues.header: P2Redundant empty state messaging in Calendar
candidateIssues.details: The right sidebar in the Calendar view displays 'Nothing scheduled.' and 'Nothing upcoming' right next to each other for the 'TODAY' section. This is redundant and adds unnecessary text.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Consolidate the message to a single, clear statement like 'No events today' and remove the secondary 'Nothing upcoming' label.

candidateIssues.header: P2Cramped typography in Research Hub 'Pulse' section
candidateIssues.details: The right-hand column in the Research Hub, specifically under 'Pulse' and 'AI Capabilities', features very small, tightly packed text that is difficult to read, reducing legibility.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the line height and font size slightly for secondary text in these widgets. Ensure adequate padding around the circular chart.

candidateIssues.header: P3Inconsistent empty state calls to action
candidateIssues.details: The 'Sources' empty state provides a clear primary button ('Go to Research Hub') to guide the user, whereas the 'Review Queue' empty state provides no actionable next step, leaving the user at a dead end.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Add a relevant call to action to the 'Review Queue' empty state, such as a link to documentation on how reviews are generated, or a button to view recent activity.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Category Breakdown' section displays 'ai_ml' as a category name. This appears to be a raw database key or internal tag rather than a user-facing label.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Map internal keys to human-readable labels (e.g., 'AI & Machine Learning') and ensure proper Title Case.

candidateIssues.details: The 'For You' feed displays '1 comments' for trending items, failing to handle singular pluralization logic.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper to display '1 comment' when the count is 1.

candidateIssues.details: The Activity screen features two primary headers ('Public Activity Feed' and 'Activity') in close proximity, creating visual clutter and redundant hierarchy.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Consolidate into a single clear header. Move the 'Filters' button to the primary header row.

candidateIssues.details: The 'Executive Synthesis' section uses the greeting 'Good morning, there'. The lowercase 'there' feels like an unaddressed placeholder or a generic fallback that lacks the professional tone of the rest of the app.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change to 'Good morning' or use the user's name if available.

candidateIssues.header: P2Repetitive Action Links in Mobile View
candidateIssues.details: In the mobile Signals view, the word 'Dashboard' is repeated four times as a text link within a single block. This creates visual noise and lacks clear affordance for what each specific dashboard link refers to.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use descriptive link text (e.g., 'View Pulse Dashboard') or use a single contextual action button at the bottom of the card.

candidateIssues.details: The search input on the mobile home screen contains a double period at the end of the placeholder text.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change to a single period or an ellipsis (...).

candidateIssues.details: The 'Source Performance' section displays the date '2026-02-23' on the right side, which is redundant given the global date picker ('Feb 23, 2026') visible immediately above it.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the redundant date string to reduce visual noise.

falsePositives.details: The 'Usage & Costs' summary card reports 'Total Items: 37', but the 'Source Performance' breakdown shows 'All Sources' as 11 items. Furthermore, the sum of individual sources (11+6+6+2+1) equals 26, creating three conflicting data points on a single screen.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Ensure the summary cards and the detailed breakdown are wired to the same data source and reflect the same filtering logic.

videoSummary: The application features a sleek, dark-mode aesthetic but suffers from information density and repetitive UI patterns that increase cognitive load. Key areas for improvement include decluttering repetitive labels in feeds, refining empty states for consistency, and reorganizing the overly long sidebar navigation to better establish hierarchy and intent.
screenSummary: The NodeBench AI interface exhibits a solid structural foundation but suffers from 'last-mile' polish issues typical of engineering-led design. Key areas for improvement include resolving significant data discrepancies in analytics, eliminating developer-centric naming conventions (snake_case), and correcting grammatical errors in pluralization. The interface also contains redundant headers and pl

aspiration.summary: NodeBench AI demonstrates a high-quality baseline with a strong 'Linear-lite' aesthetic. The craft is solid, featuring consistent radii and a professional neutral palette, but it currently suffers from 'action clutter' where too many secondary controls are always visible, and some data-dense views l

judgeDetails.reasoning: Intentional density for power users with 30+ routes is a core design principle.
Theatrical metaphors are established domain terminology for the target audience.
Overlapping labels are identified as screenshot compression artifacts in the design context.
Calm empty states without CTAs are intentional as data populates automatically.
Pluralization is fixed in code; this is a stale observation from screenshots.
Snake_case identifiers like ai_ml are unintended raw data leaks in production UI.
Redundant headers and icons are intentional navigation affordances for power users.
Context explicitly states no double periods exist; this is a screenshot misread.
Zero values and their positioning relate to placeholder data in the preview environment.
Repeated navigation links in different contexts are an intentional design choice.
Fallback greeting for missing user name is a placeholder data artifact.
Mixed date formats are intentional context-dependent design choices.
Compact touch targets are intentional and meet standards via Radix/shadcn.

realIssues.details: The Category Breakdown section displays ai_ml which appears to be a raw database key or snake_case identifier rather than a user-friendly label.
realIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Map internal keys to human-readable strings like AI & ML.

candidateIssues.details: The left sidebar exposes a massive number of top-level items, many of which lead to generic empty states. This lack of hierarchy and progressive disclosure makes the app feel cluttered and difficult to navigate, violating the principle of earned complexity.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Consolidate the navigation architecture. Group related features, hide secondary tools behind a 'More' menu or settings, and use progressive disclosure to reveal complexity only when needed.

candidateIssues.details: The Research Hub uses terms like 'Act coverage', 'Story Arc', 'Act I', 'Act II', and 'Act III'. This theatrical jargon forces users to translate metaphors into functional concepts, adding unnecessary cognitive load.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace theatrical terms with clear, descriptive labels (e.g., 'Overview', 'Key Signals', 'Deep Dives').

candidateIssues.details: The 'Pulse' chart on the right side of the Research Hub features data point labels that are extremely small and overlap, rendering them illegible and creating visual noise.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement label collision detection to hide overlapping text, or rely on interactive tooltips to display data point details on hover.

candidateIssues.details: Almost every section navigated to displays a generic 'No [item] yet' message. These dead ends fail to guide the user on how to populate the section or explain its value, missing an opportunity for craft at every scale.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Design contextual empty states that include a brief explanation of the feature's benefit and a clear primary action button (e.g., 'Create your first agent' or 'Connect a data source').

candidateIssues.details: The For You feed displays 1 comments instead of 1 comment, which breaks the sense of craft.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper function to handle singular vs. plural counts.

candidateIssues.details: The Category Breakdown section displays ai_ml which appears to be a raw database key or snake_case identifier rather than a user-friendly label.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Map internal keys to human-readable strings like AI & ML.

candidateIssues.details: The Activity page features two identical globe icons and Activity headers in close proximity, adding unnecessary visual noise.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the icon and title from the inner card if the page header already provides that context.

candidateIssues.details: The search placeholder text ends with a double period, which looks unintentional.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change file.. to file.

candidateIssues.details: In the Category Breakdown, numerical values (0) are floating at the end of progress bars without clear labels or alignment to the primary items count.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Align the secondary metric to a column header or integrate it more clearly into the row layout.

candidateIssues.details: The mobile Signals view repeats the word Dashboard as a blue link multiple times within a single text block, which is visually distracting and lacks specific context.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use more descriptive link text or move these actions to a dedicated button row.

candidateIssues.details: The Executive Synthesis greeting Good morning, there uses a lowercase there and feels like a generic fallback string.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Capitalize There or use the user's name.

candidateIssues.details: Relative dates (76d ago) are used in the desktop Signals view, while absolute dates (Feb 23, 2026) are used in the mobile view.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Standardize date formatting across all viewports for consistency.

candidateIssues.details: The Run button in the Scenarios list on mobile appears to be smaller than the recommended 44px height, making it difficult to tap accurately.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the padding and height of the Run button to meet accessibility standards.

falsePositives.details: The 'For You' feed displays '1 comments' instead of '1 comment'. This lack of attention to detail degrades the perceived quality of the interface.
falsePositives.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper function to correctly format strings based on count (e.g., count === 1 ? 'comment' : 'comments').

aspiration.summary: NodeBench AI demonstrates a high level of craft with a clean, modern aesthetic that successfully balances data density and clarity. To reach Jony Ive-grade quality, the interface needs to reduce visual noise in list views and standardize internal component padding to create a more 'calm' and premium

issues.header: P2 Interactive header buttons do not respond visually to clicks
issues.details: The 'Research', 'Assistant', and 'User Profile' buttons in the top header do not trigger any visual changes or open any expected UI elements (like drawers or menus) when clicked. The UI remains identical across all interaction states, indicating a lack of responsiveness.

issues.header: P2 'Show details' button state does not update
issues.details: After clicking 'Show details' on the Waymo card, the content expands to show 'Lead Investors', but the button label remains 'Show details' and the icon remains a down arrow instead of toggling to 'Hide details' or an up arrow.

issues.header: P2 'Show details' fails to expand content for some cards
issues.details: Clicking 'Show details' on the 'Tech Investor Battery Ventures' card does not reveal any additional information or change the card's layout, indicating a functional failure of the toggle for this specific card.

issues.header: P3 Missing active/focus visual states for header elements
issues.details: The search bar and 'Research' button do not provide visual feedback (such as a focus ring or background color change) when interacted with, making it unclear if the element is active or focused.

issues.header: P2 Interactive elements lack visual feedback and state updates
issues.details: Clicking on the 'Signals' tab, '5 MARKETS' button, and user profile menu does not trigger any visual changes, such as updating the active tab state or opening expected popovers/menus. The UI remains in the 'Overview' state despite these interactions.

issues.header: P3 Overlapping text labels in Pulse chart
issues.details: In the right-hand 'Pulse' chart, several data point labels (e.g., 'LeonLnx/taste-skill' and 'ShinMegamiBoson/Op...') overlap each other, making the text difficult to read.

issues.header: P3 [Transition bug] White flashes during page navigation
issues.details: [00:03] A brief white flash is visible between almost every page transition. This is particularly jarring in the dark mode interface and suggests a lack of smooth transition handling between routes.

issues.header: P2 [Transition bug] Generic skeleton screens cause layout shifts
issues.details: [00:01] Most routes (Home, Workspace, Benchmarks, etc.) use a generic 6-card grid skeleton screen that does not match the final page layout. This leads to significant layout shifts and a poor user experience upon content load.

realIssues.details: Primary action buttons in the Benchmarks view overflow the horizontal viewport on mobile, making the 'Run Benchmarks' action partially inaccessible.
realIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use a flex-wrap layout or prioritize actions into a meatball menu for narrow viewports.

realIssues.header: P2 'Show details' button state does not update
realIssues.details: After clicking 'Show details' on the Waymo card, the content expands to show 'Lead Investors', but the button label remains 'Show details' and the icon remains a down arrow instead of toggling to 'Hide details' or an up arrow.

candidateIssues.details: In the 'For You' feed, an article's metadata reads '1 comments'. This lack of attention to micro-copy breaks the illusion of a crafted, high-quality interface and feels unpolished.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement proper pluralization logic for dynamic counts (e.g., `count === 1 ? 'comment' : 'comments'`).

candidateIssues.details: The left sidebar exposes a massive, flat list of items categorized under 'MENU', 'MORE', and 'FILES'. This violates the principle of earned complexity, presenting a high cognitive load to the user immediately upon entering the app.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Audit the information architecture. Consolidate related tools into broader categories, utilize progressive disclosure (e.g., collapsible sections), or move less frequently used items to a secondary settings or tools menu.

candidateIssues.details: Across multiple routes (Documents, Agents, Sources, Review Queue), the empty state is nearly identical: a generic icon, 'No [item] yet', and boilerplate description text. This feels mechanical and misses an opportunity to educate the user or provide a clear, contextual call to action.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Design bespoke empty states for key areas. Explain *why* the area is empty and *how* the user can get started, ideally with an illustration and a specific primary action button.

candidateIssues.header: P2Visual clutter and lack of hierarchy in Research Hub
candidateIssues.details: The 'Latest Briefing' dashboard presents a high density of information with varying font sizes, weights, and small graphical elements (like the confidence score ring) competing for attention. It lacks a clear focal point, making it difficult to scan quickly.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Simplify the presentation. Group related metrics more clearly, increase whitespace to let elements breathe, and establish a stricter typographic hierarchy to guide the user's eye to the most critical insights first.

candidateIssues.details: The Research Hub uses terms like 'Act coverage', 'Story Arc', 'Act I - Setup', and 'Act II - Rising Action'. Unless the core user base consists of screenwriters, this metaphor is likely confusing and acts as unnecessary jargon that obscures the actual meaning of the data.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Replace these theatrical terms with clear, descriptive, and standard language that directly relates to the user's task (e.g., 'Briefing Structure', 'Introduction', 'Key Findings', 'Deep Dives').

candidateIssues.details: The 'For You' feed displays grammatically incorrect pluralization for single-count items, which undermines the perceived quality of the data synthesis.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Implement a pluralization helper function to handle conditional 's' suffixes based on count.

candidateIssues.details: Primary action buttons in the Benchmarks view overflow the horizontal viewport on mobile, making the 'Run Benchmarks' action partially inaccessible.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Use a flex-wrap layout or prioritize actions into a meatball menu for narrow viewports.

candidateIssues.details: The greeting 'Good morning, there' feels like an unpopulated template variable or a generic fallback that lacks the 'intentional' feel of the rest of the UI.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Remove the trailing 'there' or replace it with the user's name/username if available.

candidateIssues.details: The mobile signals view repeats the word 'Source' multiple times in close proximity, creating visual noise and redundant jargon.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Simplify labels to 'Lead' and 'References' or 'Links' to reduce word repetition.

candidateIssues.header: P2Unlabeled data points in Category Breakdown
candidateIssues.details: In the Analytics view, numerical values appear at the end of rows without labels or icons, making the data point's meaning ambiguous.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Add a label or icon (e.g., a 'new' or 'unread' indicator) to clarify what the trailing zero represents.

candidateIssues.details: The calendar widget in the workspace view has overlapping or extremely tight spacing between date numbers and event count indicators.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Increase the cell size or adjust the positioning of the event dot and count to prevent visual collision.

candidateIssues.header: P2Modal title does not reflect active tab
candidateIssues.details: The Settings Hub modal maintains a static title even when the user is deep-linked into 'Usage & Costs', which can cause context loss.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Update the modal header to reflect the active sidebar selection (e.g., 'Settings > Usage & Costs').

candidateIssues.details: The 'STARS' label in the Research Signals view uses all-caps, while other metadata labels like 'GitHub' and 'Neutral' use sentence or title case.
candidateIssues.suggestedFix: Suggested fix:Change 'STARS' to 'Stars' to maintain consistent typography across the card component.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Interactive header buttons do not respond visually to clicks
candidateIssues.details: The 'Research', 'Assistant', and 'User Profile' buttons in the top header do not trigger any visual changes or open any expected UI elements (like drawers or menus) when clicked. The UI remains identical across all interaction states, indicating a lack of responsiveness.

candidateIssues.header: P2 'Show details' button state does not update
candidateIssues.details: After clicking 'Show details' on the Waymo card, the content expands to show 'Lead Investors', but the button label remains 'Show details' and the icon remains a down arrow instead of toggling to 'Hide details' or an up arrow.

candidateIssues.header: P2 'Show details' fails to expand content for some cards
candidateIssues.details: Clicking 'Show details' on the 'Tech Investor Battery Ventures' card does not reveal any additional information or change the card's layout, indicating a functional failure of the toggle for this specific card.

candidateIssues.header: P3 Missing active/focus visual states for header elements
candidateIssues.details: The search bar and 'Research' button do not provide visual feedback (such as a focus ring or background color change) when interacted with, making it unclear if the element is active or focused.

candidateIssues.header: P2 Interactive elements lack visual feedback and state updates
candidateIssues.details: Clicking on the 'Signals' tab, '5 MARKETS' button, and user profile menu does not trigger any visual changes, such as updating the active tab state or opening expected popovers/menus. The UI remains in the 'Overview' state despite these interactions.

candidateIssues.header: P3 Overlapping text labels in Pulse chart
candidateIssues.details: In the right-hand 'Pulse' chart, several data point labels (e.g., 'LeonLnx/taste-skill' and 'ShinMegamiBoson/Op...') overlap each other, making the text difficult to read.

candidateIssues.header: P3 [Transition bug] White flashes during page navigation
candidateIssues.details: [00:03] A brief white flash is visible between almost every page transition. This is particularly jarring in the dark mode interface and suggests a lack of smooth transition handling between routes.

candidateIssues.header: P2 [Transition bug] Generic skeleton screens cause layout shifts
candidateIssues.details: [00:01] Most routes (Home, Workspace, Benchmarks, etc.) use a generic 6-card grid skeleton screen that does not match the final page layout. This leads to significant layout shifts and a poor user experience upon content load.

videoSummary: The application demonstrates a robust feature set but suffers from significant information architecture and polish issues. The primary navigation is overwhelming, presenting too many options at once, which increases cognitive load. Several screens rely on generic, repetitive empty states that fail to guide the user. Additionally, there are instances of unpolished micro-copy (pluralization errors)
screenSummary: The application exhibits a high level of functional complexity and a well-executed dual-theme system. However, it suffers from 'last-mile' polish issues, specifically regarding pluralization logic in dynamic feeds, horizontal overflow on mobile viewports, and inconsistent labeling conventions that occasionally introduce redundant cognitive load.

aspiration.summary: The application exhibits a high-quality baseline with a strong grasp of modern SaaS aesthetics, particularly in its use of subtle borders and neutral palettes. To reach 'Linear-grade' quality, it needs to transition from always-visible UI clutter to progressive disclosure and refine its spacing syst

issues.header: P2 [Interactive elements non-responsive]
issues.details: The 'Research' button, 'User profile' menu, and 'Configure App' button do not respond to user clicks. No menus, modals, or visual state changes occur after interaction, indicating these elements are non-functional.

issues.header: P2 [Interactive elements non-responsive]
issues.details: The 'Quick' and 'Model selection' dropdowns, the 'Autonomous Operations' section toggle, and the 'Create New Agent' (+) button do not trigger any visual state change, menus, or modals after interaction. The UI remains in its initial state despite the recorded user actions.

issues.header: P2 Missing visual feedback for interactive elements
issues.details: The UI fails to provide any visual feedback or state changes when interacting with key elements such as the search bar, 'Research' button, 'Assistant' button, and user profile icon. There are no visible focus indicators, active states, or expected UI components (like dropdown menus or popovers) appearing after these interactions, making the interface appear unresponsive.

issues.header: P2 [Dropdowns] Filter dropdowns are non-responsive
issues.details: Clicking on the 'All Time' and 'All Rounds' filter dropdowns does not trigger the expected menu or list of options. The UI remains in its initial state without any visual feedback or focus state.

issues.header: P2 Overlapping chart labels in sidebar
issues.details: In the 'Pulse' sidebar, the labels for data points on the line chart (e.g., 'LeonLnx/taste-skill', 'ShinMegamiBoson/Op...') overlap each other and the chart lines, making them illegible.

issues.header: P2 UI element overlap in sidebar
issues.details: Multiple elements in the right sidebar are overlapping: the 'Feb 2026' date badge overlaps with the 'VS. RELIA...' text, and the 'GITHUB 100%' circular graphic overlaps with the 'NOW', 'NEXT', and 'FUTURE' labels below it.

issues.header: P3 FAB obscures 'View all' link
issues.details: The floating action button (FAB) in the bottom right corner partially covers the 'View all' link for the 'SOURCES FOR TODAY' section, making it difficult to click.

issues.header: P1 [UI non-responsive to interactions]
issues.details: The interface fails to update or show any visual change after multiple user interactions, including clicking the 'Signals' tab, the 'Assistant' button, the 'collapse' icon on the synthesis card, and the user profile menu. The view remains stuck on the initial 'Overview' state in all 'After' screenshots.

issues.header: P2 [Transition] Jarring white flashes during page navigation
issues.details: [00:09] The application exhibits frequent full-screen white flashes when transitioning between different routes. This is observed throughout the session (e.g., 00:09, 00:18, 00:22, 00:31, 00:35, 00:48, 01:01, 01:18, 01:27, 02:06, 02:31, 03:10, 03:30, 04:03, 04:24, 04:43, 05:08). This significantly degrades the user experience during navigation.

judgeDetails.issue: P1Decorative system metrics exposed to user
judgeDetails.reasoning: Domain-specific metrics for AI practitioners are intentional platform features.
Intentional design pattern where empty states lack CTAs for auto-syncing data.
Theatrical terminology is part of the established Narrative Spine domain language.
Fallback greeting string used when user name data is absent in demo.

judgeDetails.issue: P2Unlabeled data points in Category Breakdown
judgeDetails.reasoning: Zero-value metrics are expected in a preview environment with mock data.
Vision models misinterpret WCAG-compliant muted text on dark backgrounds as invisible.
Tool-call eval is standard domain terminology for the target technical audience.
High information density and compact layouts are intentional for power-user scanning.
Persistent FAB across all routes is a deliberate Material Design implementation.
Codebase uses standard ellipses; double periods are artifacts of screenshot compression.

judgeDetails.issue: P2High cognitive load in Pulse sidebar
judgeDetails.reasoning: Technical granularity and GitHub paths are intentional for the target power-user audience.
Interactive elements appear broken due to missing backend data in the preview environment.
Dropdowns and creation flows require live backend connectivity to function correctly.

judgeDetails.issue: P2 Missing visual feedback for interactive elements
judgeDetails.reasoning: Unresponsiveness and missing feedback result from data-availability issues in the demo environment.
Filter menus are non-functional in the demo environment without a live backend.

judgeDetails.issue: P2 Overlapping chart labels in sidebar
judgeDetails.reasoning: Recharts labels often appear overlapped in compressed screenshots; flexbox renders correctly at resolution.

judgeDetails.issue: P2 UI element overlap in sidebar
judgeDetails.reasoning: Overlap claims are likely misreadings of compressed screenshots or intentional compact positioning.
Persistent FAB is a standard Material Design pattern for primary actions, not a bug.
Interactions appear broken due to lack of live backend data in the preview environment.

judgeDetails.issue: P2 [Transition] Jarring white flashes during page navigation
judgeDetails.reasoning: Standard React Suspense behavior during lazy-loading transitions, not a rendering bug.
